title: Summary of changes for April 2026

content:
Hey everyone!

This is the list of all the changes we've done to our projects during the month of April.

  • Wiki, documented the replacement of Pino's headstay.
  • Rabbit Waves, added a new page about Ship Lights.
  • Rejoice, a bestiary of multiset combinators.
  • Gaude, but.. in Peano's Latin.
  • Heol, implemented combinatory logic operators.
  • Hakum, added the last page for Sabotage Study.

Summer's already here, pushing us to finish a few last minute projects aboard Pino. Between all the sewing, sanding, varnishing, greasing, climbing up the mast, climbing up the mast some more, dropping tools in the water, cabinet-making(?), we're just about ready to meet it shipshape.

After taking the furler apart into a thousand little pieces and putting it all back together(without losing too many pieces), we raised our new shiny spotless jib. It looks fantastic, except that now everything else looks awfully dirty... we'll get to the rest eventually.

A few days ago, we looked at our home, with the new standing rigging, headsail, new lights, filled with food, and with less than 2 weeks to go before we cast off, and thought, okay, we have time for one last big project: ripping one of the old galley cabinets apart to rebuild it, and to install a new water tank. After ten years on Pino there are very few places aboard unknown to us, now there is sawdust on everything and we finally know what lies between the galley and cockpit locker. Before we rebuild the cabinet, we plan to pass a 100L(23 Gallon) water tank through this gap to install it in the locker.

While playtesting the new Donsol, we found a few things that could be improved with the Uxn Playdate emulator, so we fixed it and pushed a new version of Oquonie to the Catalog. The game should feel smoother, and ah, yes, there was this wall you could walk through... well, now that's fixed too.

Devine has been haunted by a cursed idea we hinted at back in February. It has come together this month into a notation called Rejoice. We've got a brief cursory description, an implementation and a few examples to demonstrate what it is. It has been fun to puzzle over what it can be used to express.

Relatedly, Giuseppe Peano whose work has directly inspired this project, wrote some of his work in a language he called as Latino Sine Flexione. The idea was to strip Classical Latin down to its simplest possible grammar while keeping its vocabulary recognizable. As a kind of tribute to Peano's work, the Rejoice documentation was translated into that language. Devine tried to emulate Peano's writing style as closely as possible, and .. yeah. Mileage may vary.

By the way, thanks to everyone who reached out with ideas for Rejoice, and helped put it all together, except for that one guy who forwarded us what ChatGPT had to say about it. Don't be that guy.

Book Club: We read Orwell's 1984, and are still reading Island by Aldous Huxley.
